oneLiner: A native macOS terminal emulator (SwiftUI + embedded Ghostty) with a workspace model and CLI built for driving Claude Code sessions.
next: Remote-workspace phase 2: forward the IPC socket + install shell integration remotely so a remote Claude pane gets live busy/idle, auto-title, and OSC-7 pwd tracking (deferred from PR #66).
Daily-drive the shipped app (now v1.5.10) and triage regressions; small follow-up candidates: Reset Name (custom→auto), worktree-leak-on-close, Escape-vs-⌘W placeholder binding.
Validate the /tian implement harness (bumped to v1.2.0 for the session model) on a real multi-slice run: async fan-out overlap + structured handoff (ADR 0003).

milestones.title: Agent delegation (/tian implement) shipped in a release

shipped.summary: Ghostty preferences are now overridable from tian Settings (⌘, → Terminal): an Option-key picker (fixes Claude Code's alt+p, which macOS otherwise composes into Unicode) plus a free-form config box, rendered into a tian-owned file loaded after ~/.config/ghostty/config so tian's UI wins while "Default" emits no line and falls through. Applying reloads the config, which Ghostty pushes to every live surface — open panes update with no relaunch. Also fixes ⌘, itself: Ghostty binds cmd+, to open_config and the surface claimed the key equivalent before the menu bar, so Settings never opened from a pane. 18 tests green, five of them running the rendered file through the real Ghostty parser (line parses, tian's file beats a conflicting user config, Default leaves the user's value in charge, bad line yields a diagnostic); verified by hand in the app.

Released v1.5.10 (build 542) — notarized + stapled, Gatekeeper-accepted, Sparkle appcast live. Ships the idle-CPU animation gating (PR #91: ~18% -> ~1.3% occluded CPU, plus a new ProcessCPUMonitor logging app_cpu: lines every 30s) and the Inspect-panel scan-runaway fix (PR #90: bounded, genuinely cancellable file-tree walk with ScanRootGuard against $HOME//Users//Volumes root scans).

Cut idle CPU (~18% -> ~1.3% occluded; 1.4% visible-idle): the three 12fps TimelineViews (BusyDotView, RainbowGlow, RainbowBorder) now pause on window occlusion via a new \.windowIsVisible environment, opacity-0 hidden sessions no longer mount their bell glow (\.sessionIsVisible), rates dropped to 8/10fps, RainbowGlow gained its missing reduceMotion branch, and SystemMonitor stops polling when no window is visible. Also adds ProcessCPUMonitor: own-process CPU/RSS sampled every 30s into the (now file-logged) perf category as `app_cpu:` lines, so the next regression is greppable instead of needing a live sample(1). Build + 1137 tianTests green. The non-git-repo git-watcher CPU issue is a separate session.

Fixed the Inspect-panel scan runaway that pegged the app at ~600% CPU / 5.4 GB RSS and hung it: a session rooted at $HOME hit an unbounded FileManager walk that ran in Task.detached (no cancellation inherited, no isCancelled polling), while a 250ms-debounced FSEventStream on a never-quiet home fired refresh() ~1/sec — so cancel-and-respawn stacked ~10 concurrent full-home walks. Walk is now bounded (20k files / 200k entries examined / depth 12 pruned), genuinely cancellable (flag polled every 256 entries under withTaskCancellationHandler), refreshes coalesce into one trailing rescan, and ScanRootGuard refuses $HOME, /, /Users, /Volumes, and /System/Volumes/Data outright in both the view model and the watcher. Build + 1155 tianTests green.
